title: Similarity-and-recency weighted local race expert
summary: Concept-drift work for sequential data shows benefit from selecting training data by both temporal proximity and feature-space similarity.
rationale: Concept-drift work for sequential data shows benefit from selecting training data by both temporal proximity and feature-space similarity. F1 strategy behavior drifts across years and differs by circuit archetype, so a single global fit may oversmooth race-specific stop logic.
implementation_hint: Build race-level meta-features from train-only data, such as estimated total laps, compound mix, degradation percentiles, and early pit-rate profile. For each validation/test race, train or weight a side expert on the nearest historical races with stronger weights for recent seasons, then blend with the global model.
expected_effect: Should help under year drift and track-specific strategy variation without relying on fixed hand-written circuit categories.
risk: Easy to leak if race descriptors are not built fold-causally; too-local sampling can reduce stability on uncommon regimes.
